title: Student Financial Aid * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Student Financial Aid * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community Blindness Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF FEDERAL STUDENT AID FOSTER YOUTH TUITION & FEE WAIVER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tuition and fee waiver available to eligible foster youth students for the equivalent of 4 years of undergraduate study 400 Maryland Avenue SW Washington, DC 20202 Eligibility: Former or current foster youth: -Must have been in ODHS or a federally recognized Oregon Tribe's care for at least 180 days after age 14 -Must not have left care before their 16th birthday Adopted former foster youth: -Must have spent at least 180 days in substitute care -Must have been adopted on or after January 1, 2012 Youth who experienced homelessness: -Must have experienced homelessness for at least 180 days between the ages of 14 and 24 -Must have received services from an ODHS-contracted organization or a host home while homeless -Must have been at least 16 years old when last receiving services Hours: Application deadline: October 1-June 30 of the award year, or the student’s last day of class for the academic year (whichever is earliest) Email (800) 433-3243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Independent Living Program (ILP) helps youth who are, or were, in foster care to become self-sufficient adults. It gives an opportunity to learn valuable skills necessary to make a successful transition from state and Tribal custody to living on their own in the community. Services include access to case management and funds for continuing education and assistance with housing. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or former foster children ages 16-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-6969 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM
